title: Cloudflare Zone & DNS Foundation — DNSSEC, strict SSL/TLS, proxied-by-default public records
description: A platform engineer onboards a public zone to Cloudflare with Hulumi's Edge Platform (v1.3.2). Target end state: ZoneFoundation enables DNSSEC and emits the DS-record / nameserver handoff outputs, sets SSL/TLS mode to strict, and applies hardened zone settings; every public application hostname is created through PublicHostname so it is proxied by default and a DNS-only record requires an explicit acknowledgement plus justification. The HulumiCloudflareHardeningPack enforces CF_DNSSEC_1 and CF_DNS_1 at preview time.
actors: AI Coding Agent (trusted, authoring Pulumi IaC)
External Attacker (untrusted; off-path DNS spoofing or origin discovery)
Contributor (semi-trusted; may add a raw DNS record for convenience)
assets: Cloudflare zone configuration and DNSSEC state
DS record and nameserver handoff outputs
Public application hostnames and their proxied/DNS-only flag
The AWS origin IP reachable behind a public hostname

stride.name: DNS response spoofing without DNSSEC
stride.description: Without DNSSEC on the public zone, an off-path attacker forges or poisons DNS responses so clients resolve the hostname to an attacker-controlled address. Mitigation: ZoneFoundation enables DNSSEC and surfaces the DS record for registrar handoff; CF_DNSSEC_1 fails preview if a public zone lacks ZoneFoundation/ZoneDnssec evidence.

stride.name: Origin IP disclosure via DNS-only public record
stride.description: A public application hostname is created DNS-only (proxied=false), so the AWS origin IP is published in DNS and discoverable from DNS history, leaking the origin and enabling WAF/bot/Access bypass. Mitigation: PublicHostname is proxied by default; a DNS-only public app record requires acknowledgeDnsOnlyExposure plus a non-empty justification, and CF_DNS_1 rejects the raw unsafe shape.

stride.name: Downgrade via weak zone SSL/TLS mode
stride.description: A zone left in Flexible (or Off) SSL/TLS mode lets traffic between Cloudflare and the origin travel unencrypted, enabling on-path tampering or downgrade. Mitigation: ZoneFoundation defaults SSL/TLS mode to strict (full strict) so origin certificates are validated end to end.

stride.name: Raw cloudflare.Record bypasses hardened defaults
stride.description: A Pulumi program uses a raw cloudflare.Record beside Hulumi components, sidestepping PublicHostname's proxied default and exposure acknowledgement. Mitigation: CF_DNS_1 rejects raw proxy-eligible public app records that set proxied=false unless a scoped suppression carries a non-empty reason.

stride.name: Unattributed zone / DNSSEC change
stride.description: A zone or DNSSEC change is made out of band and cannot be attributed. Mitigation: Pulumi state plus the Cloudflare audit trail where available; ZoneFoundation owns DNSSEC as a managed resource so drift is visible at the next preview.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.ZoneFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: DNSSEC, strict SSL/TLS mode, hardened zone settings, and nameserver/DS handoff outputs. Owning the zone as a managed resource makes DNSSEC drift visible.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.PublicHostname
recommendedComponents.rationale: Safe proxied-by-default public DNS record abstraction; a DNS-only public app record requires explicit acknowledgement plus justification, closing the origin-disclosure path.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.cloudflare.HulumiCloudflareHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (Edge Platform; rule IDs stable)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Mandatory CF_DNSSEC_1 and CF_DNS_1 rules enforce public-zone DNSSEC evidence and reject raw DNS-only public app records at preview time.
